summary: Joe Reis offers career advice for 2025 graduates, dividing guidance into timeless principles (continuous self-investment, network building, reputation management) and present-day tactics (embracing AI as a native skill, creating content to stand out). He emphasizes treating yourself like a business in an era of transactional employment while ruthlessly focusing on priorities.
key_points: Invest in continuous learning and ruthlessly prioritize the top 5 goals while saying no to everything else
Build your network non-transactionally through community involvement before you need favors
Reputation is a lifelong asset that compounds - focus on great work over short-term money
Become AI-native to capitalize on the skills gap that mid/senior people are ignoring
Create content to defeat obscurity since attention is the currency of 2025
money_quotes: The cold reality is that today's contract is very transactional - you do your job and your employer pays you. This contract can be broken at any time.
Over an extended period, your ability to learn and apply those learnings will separate you from 99% of people on this planet.
The biggest enemy most people have is obscurity.
A related irony I'm seeing is that mid and senior-level people ignore AI and pass it off as a toy. Their stubbornness is your opportunity.
If we took shortcuts and tried to make as much money as possible, we'd certainly do that. But we'd also likely take shortcuts and ruin our reputation.
key_insight: In a transactional job market where employers won't invest in you, graduates must treat themselves as businesses—building differentiated skills in AI, accumulating reputational capital through quality work, and defeating obscurity through content creation and network building.
